seba-main-title: Battle of the Stars: Science Arena
introduction-text:
Welcome to Battle of the Stars: Science Arena<\strong> – the place where you get to put the universe in your own hands. Here, everything revolves around stars, planets, and black holes<\strong>, but in a way that lets you discover, experiment, and play for yourself. <\p>

On this site, you can:

  • play with the
    life story of a single star< r>: see how it swells up, loses mass, and changes over time
  • try out how a star can end up as a
    white dwarf, neutron star, or black hole< r>
  • watch how
    stars orbit each other< r>
  • experiment with
    orbit size and eccentricity<\br> (how round or stretched out an orbit is)
  • explore what happens when stars get
    too close to each other< r>
  • find out if, and when,
    a star can “eat” a planet< r> – and what can go wrong along the way
    • <\ul><\p>

    Battle of the Stars is designed for
    everyone< r> who is curious about the universe – you don’t need to be an expert, just a bit interested (and maybe brave enough to play with supernovae and black holes).
    Ready to discover the rules of the universe – and sometimes bend them a little?
    Start the StarBattle and dive into the cosmos!<\strong><\p>

    SeBa is a software package to simulate the evolution of single and binary stars from the zero-age main-sequence up to and including remnant phases. It is valid for masses in the range 0.01 – 100 Solar masses with variable metallicity. SeBa includes prescriptions for mass loss by stellar winds, supernova and binary interactions.

    This is an online version, which allows one to run SeBa through the browser; it has limited configuration parameters compared to the stand-alone command-line tool, and can serve as a starting point to learn about stellar (binary) evolution.
mass1-tooltip: Mass of the primary star; this should be larger than or equal to the secondary star.
mass2-tooltip: Mass of the secondary star; this should be smaller than or equal to the orimary star.
ecc-tooltip: Eccentricity of the elliptical orbit: 0 is completely circular, 0.99 is nearly a straight line.
sep-tooltip: Separation between the two stars, in Solar radii.
time-tooltip: Time span of simulation in units of mega-years (= million years)
metal-tooltip: Metalliticity is the fraction of atoms with atom numbers higher than Helium compared to the total star mass (which is mainly Hydrogen and Helium)
update-directly-tooltip: Run SeBa near instantly when changing any of the input parameters
mass-field: stellar mass in units of Solar mass
radius-field: stellar radius in units of Solar radii
coremass-field: core mass in units of Solar mass
